frameworks.name: Neural Theory of Visual Attention
frameworks.one_line: vSTM capacity depends on structural connectivity between posterior thalamus and visual occipital cortices

frameworks.name: probabilistic tractography of white-matter pathways
frameworks.one_line: diffusion-weighted imaging used to estimate PT-OC connection probability across age

frameworks.name: cognitive aging of visual short-term memory
frameworks.one_line: age-related decline in vSTM capacity linked to thalamo-cortical changes

core_question: Does aging modify the association between vSTM capacity and posterior thalamus to occipital cortex structural connectivity?
key_assumptions: vSTM capacity is supported by PT-OC structural connectivity per NTVA
probabilistic tractography indexes anatomical connection probability
whole-report psychophysics yields valid vSTM capacity estimates
manipulations: PT-OC connection probability measured per individual
key_findings: vSTM capacity is reduced with aging
PT-OC connection probability is aberrant in older adults
younger adults show negative correlation between vSTM capacity and PT-OC connection probability
older adults show positive correlation between vSTM capacity and PT-OC connection probability
interpretations: age inverts the vSTM-PT-OC association, possibly reflecting white-matter property changes
thalamo-cortical structural connectivity underlies age-related vSTM decline
